title: Segmentation Fault with modified `demo2.v` from `examples/smtbmc`
body:
I was modifying `demo2.v` from `yosys`' `examples/smtbmc` directory, when I discovered an input verilog file and commands which crash `yosys` with a Segmentation Fault. This crash is reproducible on both Windows and Linux.

First, the sample file:
```
// Nothing to prove in this demo.
// Just an example for memories, vcd dumps and vlog testbench dumps.

`ifdef FORMAL
`define assume(_expr_) assume(_expr_)
`else
`define assume(_expr_)
`endif

module demo2(input clk, input [4:0] addr, output reg [31:0] data);
	reg [31:0] mem [0:31];
	always @(posedge clk)
		data <= mem[addr];

	reg [31:0] used_addr = 0;
	reg [31:0] used_dbits = 0;
	reg initstate = 1;

    always @(posedge clk) begin
        initstate <= 1;

        if (!initstate) begin
            `assume(data != 0);
            `assume((used_dbits & data) == 0);
        end
    end
endmodule
```

This is a _contrived_ minimal example to reproduce the crash. I don't think it makes sense to simulate/verify/synthesize as-is.


Next the command invocation (`./yosys -p 'read_verilog -formal test.v; prep -top demo2'`):

Lastly when dumping the log to a file on Linux, I get this on the console (guessing an exception wasn't catched that was supposed to be?):

```
terminate called after throwing an instance of 'std::out_of_range'
  what():  dict::at()
Aborted (core dumped)
```
